(e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n26\t//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n27\t//\n28\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n29\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n30\t//\n31\t// Example usage:\n32\t//\n33\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n34\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n35\t//\n36\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n37\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n38\t//\n39\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n40\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n41\t//\n42\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n43\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n44\tfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n45\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n46\t\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n47\t\n48\t\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n49\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n50\t\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n51\t\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n52\t\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n53\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n54\t\t\t\t}\n55\t\t\t\treturn parent\n56\t\t\t}\n57\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n58\t\t}\n59\t\n60\t\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n61\t\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n62\t\n63\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n64\t\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n65\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n66\t\t\t\treturn parent\n67\t\t\t}\n68\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n69\t\t}\n70\t\n71\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n72\t\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n73\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n74\t\n75\t\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n76\t\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n77\t\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n78\t\t\t}\n79\t\n80\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n81\t\t}\n82\t\n83\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n87\t// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n88\t// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n89\t//\n90\t// Parameters:\n91\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n92\t//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n93\t//   - parent: Optional parent path\n94\t//\n95\t// Returns a formatted location string.\n96\t//\n97\t// Example usage:\n98\t//\n99\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n100\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n101\t//\n102\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n103\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n104\tfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n105\t\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n106\t\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n107\t\n108\t\tif location != \"\" {\n109\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n110\t\t}\n111\t\n112\t\treturn ref\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n116\t// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n117\t//\n118\t// Parameters:\n119\t//   - fields: Slice of field names\n120\t//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n121\t//\n122\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n123\t//\n124\t// Example usage:\n125\t//\n126\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n127\t//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n128\t//\n129\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n130\t//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\n131\tfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n132\t\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n133\t\t\treturn \"\"\n134\t\t}\n135\t\n136\t\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n137\t\t\treturn fields[0]\n138\t\t}\n139\t\n140\t\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n141\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n142\t\t}\n143\t\n144\t\t// Oxford comma style\n145\t\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n146\t\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n147\t\n148\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// =============================================================================\n152\t// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n153\t// =============================================================================\n154\t\n155\t// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n156\t// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\n157\t//\n158\t// Parameters:\n159\t//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\n160\t//   - message: The core error message\n161\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n162\t//\n163\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n164\t//\n165\t// Example usage:\n166\t//\n167\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n168\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n169\t//\n170\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n171\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n172\tfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n173\t\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n174\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n175\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n176\t\n177\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n178\t\n179\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n180\t\tif errorType != \"\" {\n181\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n182\t\t}\n183\t\n184\t\t// Add field name if present\n185\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n186\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n187\t\t}\n188\t\n189\t\t// Add message\n190\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n191\t\n192\t\treturn builder.String()\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n196\t// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n197\t//\n198\t// Parameters:\n199\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n200\t//   - expected: The expected value\n201\t//   - actual: The actual value\n202\t//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n203\t//\n204\t// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n205\t//\n206\t// Example usage:\n207\t//\n208\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n209\t//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n210\t//\n211\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n212\t//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\n213\tfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n214\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n215\t\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n216\t\n217\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n218\t\n219\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n220\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n221\t\t}\n222\t\n223\t\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n224\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n225\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n226\t\t} else {\n227\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\treturn builder.String()\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n234\t// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n235\t//\n236\t// Parameters:\n237\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n238\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n239\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n240\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n241\t//\n242\t// Returns a formatted range error message.\n243\t//\n244\t// Example usage:\n245\t//\n246\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n247\t//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\n248\tfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n249\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n250\t\n251\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n252\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n253\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n254\t\t}\n255\t\n256\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n257\t\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\t// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n261\t// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n262\t//\n263\t// Parameters:\n264\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n265\t//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n266\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n267\t//\n268\t// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n269\t//\n270\t// Example usage:\n271\t//\n272\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n273\t//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\n274\tfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n275\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n276\t\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n277\t\n278\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n279\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n280\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n281\t\t}\n282\t\n283\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n284\t\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n285\t}\n286\t\n287\t// =============================================================================\n288\t// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n289\t// =============================================================================\n290\t\n291\t// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n292\t// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n293\t//\n294\t// Parameters:\n295\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n296\t//\n297\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n298\t//\n299\t// Example usage:\n300\t//\n301\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n302\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n303\t//\n304\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n305\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n306\tfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n307\t\tswitch errorType {\n308\t\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n309\t\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n310\t\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n311\t\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n312\t\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n313\t\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n314\t\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n315\t\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n316\t\tcase ErrTypeType:\n317\t\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n318\t\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n319\t\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n320\t\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n321\t\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n322\t\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n323\t\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n324\t\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n325\t\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n326\t\tdefault:\n327\t\t\treturn string(errorType)\n328\t\t}\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n332\t// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n333\t//\n334\t// Parameters:\n335\t//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n336\t//\n337\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n338\t//\n339\t// Example usage:\n340\t//\n341\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n342\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n343\t//\n344\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n345\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n346\tfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n347\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n348\t\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n349\t}\n350\t\n351\t// =============================================================================\n352\t// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n353\t// =============================================================================\n354\t\n355\t// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n356\t// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n357\t//\n358\t// Parameters:\n359\t//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n360\t//\n361\t// Returns a formatted category label.\n362\t//\n363\t// Example usage:\n364\t//\n365\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n366\t//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n367\t//\n368\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n369\t//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\n370\tfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n371\t\tswitch category {\n372\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n373\t\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n374\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n375\t\t\treturn \"Content\"\n376\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n377\t\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n378\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n379\t\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n380\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n381\t\t\treturn \"Security\"\n382\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n383\t\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n384\t\tdefault:\n385\t\t\treturn string(category)\n386\t\t}\n387\t}\n388\t\n389\t// =============================================================================\n390\t// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n391\t// =============================================================================\n392\t\n393\t// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n394\t// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n395\t//\n396\t// Parameters:\n397\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n398\t//\n399\t// Returns a formatted severity label.\n400\t//\n401\t// Example usage:\n402\t//\n403\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n404\t//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n405\t//\n406\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n407\t//\t// Returns: \"High\"\n408\tfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n409\t\tswitch severity {\n410\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n411\t\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n412\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n413\t\t\treturn \"High\"\n414\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n415\t\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n416\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n417\t\t\treturn \"Low\"\n418\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n419\t\t\treturn \"Info\"\n420\t\tdefault:\n421\t\t\treturn string(severity)\n422\t\t}\n423\t}\n424\t\n425\t// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n426\t// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n427\t//\n428\t// Parameters:\n429\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n430\t//\n431\t// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n432\t//\n433\t// Example usage:\n434\t//\n435\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n436\t//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n437\t//\n438\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n439\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\n440\tfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n441\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n442\t\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n443\t\n444\t\tif indicator != \"\" {\n445\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n446\t\t}\n447\t\n448\t\treturn label\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n452\t// Uses emoji indicators for clear visual distinction:\n453\t// - 🚨 (Critical): Alert/ sirens for critical issues\n454\t// - ⚠️ (High): Warning sign for high severity\n455\t// - ⚡ (Medium): Lightning bolt for medium severity\n456\t// - ℹ️ (Low): Info for low severity issues\n457\t// - 💡 (Info): Light bulb for informational messages\n458\t// - ❓ (Unknown): Question mark for unknown severity\n459\tfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n460\t\tswitch severity {\n461\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n462\t\t\treturn \"🚨\"\n463\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n464\t\t\treturn \"⚠️\"\n465\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n466\t\t\treturn \"⚡\"\n467\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n468\t\t\treturn \"ℹ️\"\n469\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n470\t\t\treturn \"💡\"\n471\t\tdefault:\n472\t\t\treturn \"❓\"\n473\t\t}\n474\t}\n475\t\n476\t// =============================================================================\n477\t// EXPECTED VS ACTUAL FORMATTING\n478\t// =============================================================================\n479\t\n480\t// FormatExpectedActual formats an ExpectedActual struct into a readable string.\n481\t// This function handles different value types and formats them side-by-side.\n482\t// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty (both values nil).\n483\t//\n484\t// Parameters:\n485\t//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n486\t//\n487\t// Returns a formatted string showing expected vs actual values, or empty string if nil/empty.\n488\t//\n489\t// Example usage:\n490\t//\n491\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n492\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n493\t//\t// Returns: \"expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found)\"\n494\t//\n495\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(\"test@example.com\", \"invalid\")\n496\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n497\t//\t// Returns: \"expected: 'test@example.com', actual: 'invalid'\"\n498\tfunc FormatExpectedActual(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n499\t\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n500\t\t\treturn \"\"\n501\t\t}\n502\t\n503\t\tvar expectedStr, actualStr string\n504\t\n505\t\t// Format expected value\n506\t\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n507\t\t\tswitch exp := ea.Expected.(type) {\n508\t\t\tcase int:\n509\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp))\n510\t\t\tcase []int:\n511\t\t\t\tparts := make([]string, len(exp))\n512\t\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n513\t\t\t\t\tparts[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code))\n514\t\t\t\t}\n515\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"one of [%s]\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n516\t\t\tcase string:\n517\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", exp)\n518\t\t\tcase float64:\n519\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", exp)\n520\t\t\tcase []string:\n521\t\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(exp))\n522\t\t\t\tfor i, s := range exp {\n523\t\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n524\t\t\t\t}\n525\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n526\t\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n527\t\t\t\texpectedStr = formatMapValue(exp)\n528\t\t\tcase []interface{}:\n529\t\t\t\texpectedStr = formatSliceValue(exp)\n530\t\t\tdefault:\n531\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", exp)\n532\t\t\t}\n533\t\t}\n534\t\n535\t\t// Format actual value\n536\t\tif ea.HasActual() {\n537\t\t\tswitch act := ea.Actual.(type) {\n538\t\t\tcase int:\n539\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act))\n540\t\t\tcase string:\n541\t\t\t\t// Truncate long strings\n542\t\t\t\tif len(act) > 100 {\n543\t\t\t\t\tact = act[:100] + \"...\"\n544\t\t\t\t}\n545\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", act)\n546\t\t\tcase float64:\n547\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", act)\n548\t\t\tcase []string:\n549\t\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(act))\n550\t\t\t\tfor i, s := range act {\n551\t\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n552\t\t\t\t}\n553\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n554\t\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n555\t\t\t\tactualStr = formatMapValue(act)\n556\t\t\tcase []interface{}:\n557\t\t\t\tactualStr = formatSliceValue(act)\n558\t\t\tdefault:\n559\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", act)\n560\t\t\t}\n561\t\t}\n562\t\n563\t\t// Build the formatted string\n564\t\tvar parts []string\n565\t\tif expectedStr != \"\" {\n566\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", expectedStr))\n567\t\t}\n568\t\tif actualStr != \"\" {\n569\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", actualStr))\n570\t\t}\n571\t\n572\t\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n573\t\t\treturn \"\"\n574\t\t}\n575\t\n576\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n577\t}\n578\t\n579\t// formatMapValue formats a map value for display in error messages.\n580\tfunc formatMapValue(m map[string]interface{}) string {\n581\t\tif len(m) == 0 {\n582\t\t\treturn \"{}\"\n583\t\t}\n584\t\n585\t\tvar pairs []string\n586\t\tfor k, v := range m {\n587\t\t\tpairs = append(pairs, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", k, v))\n588\t\t}\n589\t\n590\t\t// Limit output for large maps\n591\t\tif len(pairs) > 5 {\n592\t\t\tpairs = pairs[:5]\n593\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s, ...}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n594\t\t}\n595\t\n596\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n597\t}\n598\t\n599\t// formatSliceValue formats a slice value for display in error messages.\n600\tfunc formatSliceValue(s []interface{}) string {\n601\t\tif len(s) == 0 {\n602\t\t\treturn \"[]\"\n603\t\t}\n604\t\n605\t\tvar items []string\n606\t\tfor _, v := range s {\n607\t\t\titems = append(items, fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", v))\n608\t\t}\n609\t\n610\t\t// Limit output for large slices\n611\t\tif len(items) > 5 {\n612\t\t\titems = items[:5]\n613\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s, ...]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n614\t\t}\n615\t\n616\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n617\t}\n618\t\n619\t// FormatExpectedActualInline formats ExpectedActual values inline (compact format).\n620\t// This is useful for embedding in error messages where space is limited.\n621\t// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty.\n622\t//\n623\t// Parameters:\n624\t//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n625\t//\n626\t// Returns a compact formatted string.\n627\t//\n628\t// Example usage:\n629\t//\n630\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n631\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActualInline(ea)\n632\t//\t// Returns: \"(expected 200, got 404)\"\n633\tfunc FormatExpectedActualInline(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n634\t\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n635\t\t\treturn \"\"\n636\t\t}\n637\t\n638\t\tvar parts []string\n639\t\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n640\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v\", ea.Expected))\n641\t\t}\n642\t\tif ea.HasActual() {\n643\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"got %v\", ea.Actual))\n644\t\t}\n645\t\n646\t\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n647\t\t\treturn \"\"\n648\t\t}\n649\t\n650\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"(%s)\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n651\t}\n652\t\n653\t// =============================================================================\n654\t// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n655\t// =============================================================================\n656\t\n657\t// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n658\t// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n659\t// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n660\t//\n661\t// Parameters:\n662\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n663\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n664\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n665\t//\n666\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n667\t//\n668\t// Example usage:\n669\t//\n670\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n671\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n672\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n673\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n674\t//\t}\n675\t//\n676\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, nil)\n677\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n678\t//\n679\t//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n680\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, ctx)\n681\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\n682\tfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n683\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n684\t\n685\t\t// Add severity if requested\n686\t\tif includeSeverity {\n687\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n688\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n689\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n690\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n691\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n692\t\t\t} else {\n693\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n694\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n695\t\t\t}\n696\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n697\t\t}\n698\t\n699\t\t// Add error type and field\n700\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n701\t\n702\t\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n703\t\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n704\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n705\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n706\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n707\t\t\t}\n708\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n709\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n710\t\t\t}\n711\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n712\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n713\t\t\t}\n714\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n715\t\t}\n716\t\n717\t\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n718\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n719\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n720\t\t}\n721\t\n722\t\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n723\t\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n724\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n725\t\t\tvar contextParts []string\n726\t\n727\t\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n728\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n729\t\t\t}\n730\t\n731\t\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n732\t\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n733\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n734\t\t\t}\n735\t\n736\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n737\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n738\t\t}\n739\t\n740\t\t// Add suggestions if present\n741\t\tif len(err.Suggestions) > 0 {\n742\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\"\\nSuggestions:\")\n743\t\t\tfor _, suggestion := range err.Suggestions {\n744\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n  - %s\", suggestion))\n745\t\t\t}\n746\t\t}\n747\t\n748\t\treturn builder.String()\n749\t}\n750\t\n751\t// FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual creates a comprehensive, formatted error message\n752\t// with optional ExpectedActual parameter for value comparison. This extends FormatValidationErrorFull\n753\t// to support structured expected vs actual value display.\n754\t//\n755\t// Parameters:\n756\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n757\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n758\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n759\t//   - expectedActual: Optional ExpectedActual for structured value comparison display\n760\t//\n761\t// Returns a formatted error message string with optional expected/actual comparison.\n762\t//\n763\t// The ExpectedActual parameter takes precedence over err.Expected/err.Actual when provided.\n764\t// If expectedActual is nil or empty, the function falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual.\n765\t// If both are empty, no value comparison is included.\n766\t//\n767\t// Example usage:\n768\t//\n769\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n770\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n771\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n772\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n773\t//\t}\n774\t//\n775\t//\t// With ExpectedActual parameter\n776\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n777\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ea)\n778\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found))\"\n779\t//\n780\t//\t// Without ExpectedActual parameter (falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual)\n781\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ExpectedActual{})\n782\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n783\tfunc FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext, expectedActual ExpectedActual) ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n790\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n791\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n792\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n793\t\t\t} else {\n794\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n795\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n796\t\t\t}\n797\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n798\t\t}\n799\t\n800\t\t// Add error type and field\n801\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n802\t\n803\t\t// Add expected/actual values - prefer ExpectedActual parameter if provided and non-empty\n804\t\tif !expectedActual.IsEmpty() {\n805\t\t\tformattedEA := FormatExpectedActual(expectedActual)\n806\t\t\tif formattedEA != \"\" {\n807\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (%s)\", formattedEA))\n808\t\t\t}\n809\t\t} else if err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n810\t\t\t// Fall back to err.Expected/err.Actual\n811\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n812\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n813\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n814\t\t\t}\n815\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n816\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n817\t\t\t}\n818\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n819\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n820\t\t\t}\n821\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n822\t\t}\n823\t\n824\t\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n825\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n826\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n827\t\t}\n828\t\n829\t\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n830\t\tif FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n840\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n841\t\t\t}\n842\t\n843\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n844\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n845\t\t}\n846\t\n847\t\t// Add suggestions if present\n848\t\tif =============================================================================\n887\t\n888\t// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n889\t// This is useful for displaying [High] [format] password: Invalid format (location: line 10)\n961\tfunc FormatErrorListWithContext(errors []ValidationError, contexts []*ValidationErrorContext, includeSeverity bool) string {\n962\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n963\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n964\t\t}\n965\t\n966\t\tvar lines []string\n967\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n968\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n969\t\n970\t\t\tvar context *ValidationErrorContext\n971\t\t\tif contexts != nil && i < len(contexts) {\n972\t\t\t\tcontext = contexts[i]\n973\t\t\t}\n974\t\n975\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, context)\n976\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n977\t\t}\n978\t\n979\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n980\t}\n981\t\n982\t// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n983\t// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n984\t//\n985\t// Parameters:\n986\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n987\t//\n988\t// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n989\t//\n990\t// Example usage:\n991\t//\n992\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n993\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n994\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n995\t//\t}, {\n996\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n997\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n998\t//\t}}\n999\t//\n1000\t//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n1001\t//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\n1002\tfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n1003\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n1004\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n1005\t\t}\n1006\t\n1007\t\t// Count errors by type\n1008\t\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n1009\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n1010\t\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n1011\t\t}\n1012\t\n1013\t\t// Build summary parts\n1014\t\tvar parts []string\n1015\t\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n1016\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n1017\t\t}\n1018\t\n1019\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n1020\t}\n1021\t\n1022\t// =============================================================================\n1023\t// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n1024\t// =============================================================================\n1025\t\n1026\t// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n1027\t// without the parent parameter.\n1028\t//\n1029\t// Parameters:\n1030\t//   - maxLength.\n1132\t//\n1133\t// Parameters:\n1134\t//   - value: The value to truncate\n1135\t//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n1136\t//\n1137\t// Returns a truncated value string TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n1144\t\tif maxLength < 3 {\n1145\t\t\tmaxLength = 3\n1146\t\t}\n1147\t\n1148\t\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n1149\t\t\treturn value\n1150\t\t}\n1151\t\n1152\t\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n1153\t}\n1154\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"86d21a9e-32c5-4f35-b611-8c7940394de5","uuid":"dec59261-5059-4e60-8c3d-7f75f82e45fd","timestamp":"2026-07-15T20:10:10.618Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/validate/error_formatting.go","content":"package validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n/*\nError Formatting Helpers\n\nThis file provides helper functions for formatting validation errors consistently.\nThese helpers work with the ErrorType enum, ErrorCategory, and ErrorSeverity types\nto produce human-readable, well-formatted error messages.\n*/\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n// This ensures field names are consistently formatted across error messages.\n// Supports nested field paths and array index formatting.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The name of the field (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\nfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n\n\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n\n\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\n\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n\t}\n\n\treturn normalizedFieldName\n}\n\n// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n\n\tif location != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n\t}\n\n\treturn ref\n}\n\n// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\nfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n\t\treturn fields[0]\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Oxford comma style\n\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\nfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add error type in brackets\n\tif errorType != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field name if present\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - expected: The expected value\n//   - actual: The actual value\n//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n\t} else {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted range error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n\tswitch errorType {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeType:\n\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(errorType)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n//\n// Returns a formatted category label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\nfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n\tswitch category {\n\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n\tcase CategoryContent:\n\t\treturn \"Content\"\n\tcase CategoryValidation:\n\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n\tcase CategorySecurity:\n\t\treturn \"Security\"\n\tcase CategoryCustom:\n\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(category)\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a formatted severity label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"High\"\nfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"High\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"Low\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"Info\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(severity)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\nfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n\n\tif indicator != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n\t}\n\n\treturn label\n}\n\n// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n// Uses emoji indicators for clear visual distinction:\n// - 🚨 (Critical): Alert/ sirens for critical issues\n// - ⚠️ (High): Warning sign for high severity\n// - ⚡ (Medium): Lightning bolt for medium severity\n// - ℹ️ (Low): Info for low severity issues\n// - 💡 (Info): Light bulb for informational messages\n// - ❓ (Unknown): Question mark for unknown severity\nfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"🚨\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"⚠️\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"⚡\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"ℹ️\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"💡\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"❓\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// EXPECTED VS ACTUAL FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatExpectedActual formats an ExpectedActual struct into a readable string.\n// This function handles different value types and formats them side-by-side.\n// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty (both values nil).\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n//\n// Returns a formatted string showing expected vs actual values, or empty string if nil/empty.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n//\t// Returns: \"expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found)\"\n//\n//\tea := NewExpectedActual(\"test@example.com\", \"invalid\")\n//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n//\t// Returns: \"expected: 'test@example.com', actual: 'invalid'\"\nfunc FormatExpectedActual(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar expectedStr, actualStr string\n\n\t// Format expected value\n\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n\t\tswitch exp := ea.Expected.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp))\n\t\tcase []int:\n\t\t\tparts := make([]string, len(exp))\n\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n\t\t\t\tparts[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"one of [%s]\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n\t\tcase string:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", exp)\n\t\tcase float64:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", exp)\n\t\tcase []string:\n\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(exp))\n\t\t\tfor i, s := range exp {\n\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = formatMapValue(exp)\n\t\tcase []interface{}:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = formatSliceValue(exp)\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", exp)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Format actual value\n\tif ea.HasActual() {\n\t\tswitch act := ea.Actual.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act))\n\t\tcase string:\n\t\t\t// Truncate long strings\n\t\t\tif len(act) > 100 {\n\t\t\t\tact = act[:100] + \"...\"\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", act)\n\t\tcase float64:\n\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", act)\n\t\tcase []string:\n\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(act))\n\t\t\tfor i, s := range act {\n\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n\t\t\tactualStr = formatMapValue(act)\n\t\tcase []interface{}:\n\t\t\tactualStr = formatSliceValue(act)\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", act)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build the formatted string\n\tvar parts []string\n\tif expectedStr != \"\" {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", expectedStr))\n\t}\n\tif actualStr != \"\" {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", actualStr))\n\t}\n\n\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n}\n\n// formatMapValue formats a map value for display in error messages.\nfunc formatMapValue(m map[string]interface{}) string {\n\tif len(m) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"{}\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar pairs []string\n\tfor k, v := range m {\n\t\tpairs = append(pairs, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", k, v))\n\t}\n\n\t// Limit output for large maps\n\tif len(pairs) > 5 {\n\t\tpairs = pairs[:5]\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s, ...}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n}\n\n// formatSliceValue formats a slice value for display in error messages.\nfunc formatSliceValue(s []interface{}) string {\n\tif len(s) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"[]\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar items []string\n\tfor _, v := range s {\n\t\titems = append(items, fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", v))\n\t}\n\n\t// Limit output for large slices\n\tif len(items) > 5 {\n\t\titems = items[:5]\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s, ...]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n}\n\n// FormatExpectedActualInline formats ExpectedActual values inline (compact format).\n// This is useful for embedding in error messages where space is limited.\n// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n//\n// Returns a compact formatted string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActualInline(ea)\n//\t// Returns: \"(expected 200, got 404)\"\nfunc FormatExpectedActualInline(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar parts []string\n\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v\", ea.Expected))\n\t}\n\tif ea.HasActual() {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"got %v\", ea.Actual))\n\t}\n\n\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"(%s)\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, nil)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, ctx)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity if requested\n\tif includeSeverity {\n\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add error type and field\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n\n\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tvar contextParts []string\n\n\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add suggestions if present\n\tif len(err.Suggestions) > 0 {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\"\\nSuggestions:\")\n\t\tfor _, suggestion := range err.Suggestions {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n  - %s\", suggestion))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual creates a comprehensive, formatted error message\n// with optional ExpectedActual parameter for value comparison. This extends FormatValidationErrorFull\n// to support structured expected vs actual value display.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n//   - expectedActual: Optional ExpectedActual for structured value comparison display\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string with optional expected/actual comparison.\n//\n// The ExpectedActual parameter takes precedence over err.Expected/err.Actual when provided.\n// If expectedActual is nil or empty, the function falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual.\n// If both are empty, no value comparison is included.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// With ExpectedActual parameter\n//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ea)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found))\"\n//\n//\t// Without ExpectedActual parameter (falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual)\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ExpectedActual{})\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext, expectedActual ExpectedActual) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity if requested\n\tif includeSeverity {\n\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add error type and field\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n\n\t// Add expected/actual values - prefer ExpectedActual parameter if provided and non-empty\n\tif !expectedActual.IsEmpty() {\n\t\tformattedEA := FormatExpectedActual(expectedActual)\n\t\tif formattedEA != \"\" {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (%s)\", formattedEA))\n\t\t}\n\t} else if err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t// Fall back to err.Expected/err.Actual\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tvar contextParts []string\n\n\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add suggestions if present\n\tif len(err.Suggestions) > 0 {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\"\\nSuggestions:\")\n\t\tfor _, suggestion := range err.Suggestions {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n  - %s\", suggestion))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//\n// Returns a brief error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n\t}\n\n\treturn err.ErrorType\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n//\t// Returns:\n//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\nfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\tfor i, err := range errors {\n\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, nil)\n\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// FormatErrorListWithContext formats multiple errors into a readable list with context information.\n// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures with additional context for each error.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//   - contexts: Slice of ValidationErrorContext structs, one per error (can contain nil values)\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list string.\n//\n// If contexts is nil or shorter than errors, missing contexts are treated as nil (no context).\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tcontexts := []*ValidationErrorContext{\n//\t    NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"}),\n//\t    NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 10\"),\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorListWithContext(errors, contexts, true)\n//\t// Returns:\n//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\n//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format (location: line 10)\nfunc FormatErrorListWithContext(errors []ValidationError, contexts []*ValidationErrorContext, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\tfor i, err := range errors {\n\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n\n\t\tvar context *ValidationErrorContext\n\t\tif contexts != nil && i < len(contexts) {\n\t\t\tcontext = contexts[i]\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, context)\n\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n//\n// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\nfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Count errors by type\n\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n\t}\n\n\t// Build summary parts\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n// without the parent parameter.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocation(\"email\", \"line 5\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email at line 5\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocation(fieldName string, location string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, \"\")\n}\n\n// FormatFieldList is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldListWith\n// with \"and\" as the default conjunction.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldList([]string{\"email\", \"password\"})\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\nfunc FormatFieldList(fields []string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldListWith(fields, \"and\")\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorToString is an alias for FormatValidationErrorFull without context.\n// This provides a more explicit name for converting a ValidationError to a string.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\nfunc FormatValidationErrorToString(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, nil)\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorWithContext creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError\n// with optional context information. This is a convenience function that simplifies the common case\n// of formatting an error with context.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithContext(err, true, ctx)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorWithContext(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, context)\n}\n\n// TruncateValue is an alias for TruncateString for compatibility.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to truncate\n//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n//\n// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\nfunc TruncateValue(value string, maxLength int) string {\n\treturn TruncateString(value, maxLength)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// QuoteValue wraps a value in quotes for display in error messages.\n// Empty values return \"(empty)\" instead of quotes.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to quote\n//\n// Returns a quoted value string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"'email'\"\n//\n//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(empty)\"\nfunc QuoteValue(value string) string {\n\tif value == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"(empty)\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", value)\n}\n\n// TruncateString truncates a string value to a maximum length for display.\n// This is useful for keeping error messages concise.\n// Unlike TruncateValue in format_helpers.go, this takes a string and maxLength.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to truncate\n//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n//\n// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\nfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n\tif maxLength < 3 {\n\t\tmaxLength = 3\n\t}\n\n\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n\t\treturn value\n\t}\n\n\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n}\n","numLines":1154,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1154}}}

types.\n79\tfunc (vec ValidationErrorContext) Validate() error {\n80\t\t// All fields are optional, so a ValidationErrorContext is always valid\n81\t\treturn nil\n82\t}\n83\t\n84\t// ExpectedActual represents the comparison between expected and actual values in validation.\n85\t// This struct provides a structured way to represent what was expected versus what was\n86\t// actually received expected and actual values differ.\n120\t// Returns true if both Expected and Actual are set and they are not equal.\n121\t// Note: This uses simple equality comparison and may not work correctly for appropriately\n128\t\tswitch exp := ea.Expected.(type) {\n129\t\tcase int:\n130\t\t\tif act, ok := ea.Actual.(int); ok {\n131\t\t\t\treturn exp != act\n132\t\t\t}\n133\t\tcase string:\n134\t\t\tif use simple comparison\n150\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", ea.Expected) != fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", ea.Actual)\n151\t}\n152\t\n153\t// IsEmpty checks if both Expected and Actual values are unset.\n154\t// nil\n214\t}\n215\t\n216\t// Suggestion represents a single actionable recommendation for resolving a validation error.\n217\t// This struct provides a structured way to present hints and guidance to Priority: \"high\",\n225\t//\t    Category: \"documentation\",\n226\t//\t}\n227\t//\tif suggestion.IsActionable() {\n228\t//\t    fmt.Println(suggestion.Message)\n229\t//\t}\n230\ttype Suggestion suggestion.\n234\t\tMessage string `json:\"message,omitempty\"`\n235\t\n236\t\t// Priority indicates the importance or urgency of this suggestion.\n237\t\t// Values: \"high\", \"medium\", \"low\".
