Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n363\t\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n364\t\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n365\t}\n366\t\n367\t// Example 15: Reusable validation helper\n368\tfunc ExampleValidationError_reusableHelper() {\n369\t\t// Define a reusable helper function\n370\t\tcheckStatusCode := func(expected, actual int, operation string) error {\n371\t\t\tif expected != actual {\n372\t\t\t\treturn FormatStatusCodeError(expected, actual, operation)\n373\t\t\t}\n374\t\t\treturn nil\n375\t\t}\n376\t\n377\t\t// Use the helper in various scenarios\n378\t\tscenarios := []struct {\n379\t\t\tname     string\n380\t\t\texpected int\n381\t\t\tactual   int\n382\t\t\tcontext  string\n383\t\t}{\n384\t\t\t{\"User lookup\", 200, 200, \"GET /api/users/123\"},\n385\t\t\t{\"Create user\", 201, 409, \"POST /api/users\"},\n386\t\t\t{\"Delete user\", 204, 403, \"DELETE /api/users/123\"},\n387\t\t}\n388\t\n389\t\tfor _, scenario := range scenarios {\n390\t\t\tif err := checkStatusCode(scenario.expected, scenario.actual, scenario.context); err != nil {\n391\t\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"%s failed: %v\\n\", scenario.name, err)\n392\t\t\t} else {\n393\t\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"%s succeeded\\n\", scenario.name)\n394\t\t\t}\n395\t\t}\n396\t\n397\t\t// Output:\n398\t\t// User lookup succeeded\n399\t\t// Create user failed: status_code validation failed\n400\t\t//   Expected: 201 (Created)\n401\t\t//   Actual:   409 (Conflict)\n402\t\t//   Context:  POST /api/users\n403\t\t//   Suggestions:\n404\t\t//     - Check if a resource with this identifier already exists\n405\t\t//     - Verify unique constraints on the resource\n406\t\t//     - Consider using PUT instead of POST for updates\n407\t\t// Delete user failed: status_code validation failed\n408\t\t//   Expected: 204 (No Content)\n409\t\t//   Actual:   403 (Forbidden)\n410\t\t//   Context:  DELETE /api/users/123\n411\t\t//   Suggestions:\n412\t\t//     - JSON parsing failed at position 56\n\t//     - Unterminated string value\n\t//     - Missing closing brace }\n\t//   Suggestions:\n\t//     - Verify JSON is properly formatted\n\t//     - Check for special characters that need escaping\n\t//     - Use JSON linter to validate format\n\t//     - Review request body encoding\n}\n\n// Example 11: Accessing ValidationError fields programmatically\nfunc ExampleValidationError_programmaticAccess() {\n\terr := FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users\")\n\n\t// Access fields for programmatic handling\n\tvalidationErr := err\n\tif validationErr.ErrorType == \"status_code\" {\n\t\tif actualCode, ok := validationErr.Actual.(int); ok {\n\t\t\tif actualCode == 404 {\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Println(\"Resource not found - check endpoint and ID\n\t// Type: status_code\n\t// Expected: 200\n\t// Actual: 404\n\t// Has 3 suggestions\n}\n\n// Example 12: ValidationError as error interface\nfunc ExampleValidationError_asError() {\n\tvar err error\n\terr = FormatStatusCodeError(200, 500, \"GET /api/data\")\n\n\t// ValidationError implements error interface\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tfmt.Println(\"Error occurred:\", err.Error())\n\t}\n\n\t// Type assertion to access ValidationError\n\tif validationErr, ok := err.(ValidationError); ok {\n\t\tfmt.Printf(\"Validation type: %s\\n\", validationErr.ErrorType)\n\t\tfmt.Printf(\"Context: %s\\n\", validationErr.Context)\n\t}\n\n\t// Output:\n\t// Error occurred: status_code validation failed\n\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n\t//   Actual:   500 (Internal Server Error)\n\t//   Context:  GET /api/data\n\t//   Suggestions:\n\t//     - Implement retry logic with exponential backoff\n\t//     - Check service status page for ongoing issues\n\t//     - Contact support if the issue persists\n\t// Validation type: status_code\n\t// Context: GET /api/data\n}\n\n// Example 13: Creating validation errors in tests\nfunc ExampleValidationError_inTest() {\n\t// Simulate a test that expects status 200 but gets 401\n\texpectedStatus := 200\n\tactualStatus := 401\n\tcontext := \"GET /api/protected\"\n\n\tif expectedStatus != actualStatus {\n\t\terr := FormatStatusCodeError(expectedStatus, actualStatus, context)\n\t\tfmt.Printf(\"Test failed: %v\\n\", err.Error())\n\t}\n\n\t// Output:\n\t// Test failed: status_code validation failed\n\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n\t//   Actual:   401 (Unauthorized)\n\t//   Context:  GET /api/protected\n\t//   Suggestions:\n\t//     - All optional fields are truly optional - existing code will continue to compile and work without modification.\n6\t\n7\t## Core Formatting Functions\n8\t\n9\t### FormatValidationErrorWithDetails\n10\t\n11\tThis is the most comprehensive error formatting function. All parameters after the first 5 are optional:\n12\t\n13\t**Required Parameters:**\n14\t- `validationType` string - The category of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\")\n15\t- `expected` interface{} - The expected value\n16\t- `actual` interface{} - The actual value received\n17\t- `context` string - Additional context about the validation operation\n18\t- `responseSnippet` string - Truncated response excerpt for debugging\n19\t\n20\t**Optional Parameters:**\n21\t- `fieldName` string - The specific field name where the error was found (default: \"\")\n22\t- `location` string - Location information (default: \"\")\n23\t- `relatedFields` []string - Related field names for context (default: nil)\n24\t- `patternDetails` string - Pattern matching failure details (default: \"\")\n25\t- `rangeInfo` string - Range validation information (default: \"\")\n26\t- `validationDetails` []string - Additional validation-specific details (default: nil)\n27\t- `customSuggestions` ...string - Custom suggestions for fixing the error (default: auto-generated)\n28\t\n29\t**Example Usage:**\n30\t\n31\t```go\n32\t// Minimal call with only required parameters\n33\terr := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n34\t    \"status_code\",    // validationType\n35\t    200,              // expected\n36\t    404,              // actual\n37\t    \"GET /api/users\", // context\n38\t    `{\"error\": \"not_found\"}`, // responseSnippet\n39\t    \"\",               // fieldName (optional, empty)\n40\t    \"\",               // location (optional, empty)\n41\t    nil,              // relatedFields (optional, nil)\n42\t    \"\",               // patternDetails (optional, empty)\n43\t    \"\",               // rangeInfo (optional, empty)\n44\t    nil,              // validationDetails (optional, nil)\n45\t)\n46\t\n47\t// Same call with optional parameters omitted for brevity\n48\terr := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n49\t    \"status_code\", 200, 404, \"GET /api/users\", `{\"error\": \"not_found\"}`,\n50\t    \"\", \"\", nil, \"\", \"\", nil,\n51\t)\n52\t\n53\t// Full call with all optional parameters\n54\terr := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n55\t    \"status_code\", 200, 404, \"GET /api/users\", `{\"error\": \"not_found\"}`,\n56\t    \"response.status\",                        // fieldName\n57\t    \"line 15 in api_client.go\",              // location\n58\t    []string{\"error_code\", \"response_body\"}, // relatedFields\n59\t    \"expected status code 2xx, got 404\",      // patternDetails\n60\t    \"200-299 (Success)\",                     // rangeInfo\n61\t    []string{\"Checked status code\", \"Expected 2xx\", \"Got 404\"}, // validationDetails\n62\t    \"Verify the resource exists\", \"Check authentication\", \"Review API endpoint\", // customSuggestions\n63\t)\n64\t```\n65\t\n66\t### FormatValidationErrorFull\n67\t\n68\tThis function formats a ValidationError struct with optional context:\n69\t\n70\t**Parameters:**\n71\t- `err` ValidationError - The validation error to format (required)\n72\t- `includeSeverity` bool - Whether to include severity information (required)\n73\t- `context` *ValidationErrorContext - Optional context information (optional, can be nil)\n74\t\n75\t**Example Usage:**\n76\t\n77\t```go\n78\t// Without optional context\n79\terr := ValidationError{\n80\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n81\t    Message:   \"Field is required\",\n82\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n83\t}\n84\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, nil)\n85\t\n86\t// With optional context\n87\tctx := &ValidationErrorContext{\n88\t    Location: \"line 5\",\n89\t    RelatedFields: []string{\"email_confirmation\"},\n90\t}\n91\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, ctx)\n92\t```\n93\t\n94\t### FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual\n95\t\n96\tThis function extends FormatValidationErrorFull with optional expected/actual value comparison:\n97\t\n98\t**Parameters:**\n99\t- `err` ValidationError - The validation error to format (required)\n100\t- `includeSeverity` bool - Whether to include severity information (required)\n101\t- `context` *ValidationErrorContext - Optional context information (optional, can be nil)\n102\t- `expectedActual` ExpectedActual - Optional expected/actual comparison (optional, can be empty)\n103\t\n104\t**Example Usage:**\n105\t\n106\t```go\n107\t// Without optional parameters\n108\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ExpectedActual{})\n109\t\n110\t// With optional expected/actual comparison\n111\tea := ExpectedActual{Expected: 200, Actual: 404}\n112\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ea)\n113\t\n114\t// With both context and expected/actual\n115\tctx := &ValidationErrorContext{Location: \"line 20\"}\n116\tea := ExpectedActual{Expected: []int{200, 201, 204}, Actual: 404}\n117\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, ctx, ea)\n118\t```\n119\t\n120\t## ValidationFormatter Builder\n121\t\n122\tThe `ValidationFormatter` provides a builder pattern for creating validation errors ergonomically:\n123\t\n124\t```go\n125\t// Minimal usage - only required fields\n126\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n127\t    WithExpected(200).\n128\t    WithActual(404).\n129\t    Format()\n130\t\n131\t// Full usage - all optional fields\n132\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n133\t    WithExpected(200).\n134\t    WithActual(404).\n135\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n136\t    WithResponseSnippet(`{\"error\": \"not_found\"}`).\n137\t    WithFieldName(\"response.status\").\n138\t    WithPatternDetails(\"expected status code 2xx, got 404\").\n139\t    WithRangeInfo(\"200-299 (Success)\").\n140\t    WithValidationDetails(\"Checked status code\", \"Expected 2xx\", \"Got 404\").\n141\t    WithSuggestions(\"Verify the resource exists\", \"Check authentication\").\n142\t    Format()\n143\t```\n144\t\n145\t## Backward Compatibility Verification\n146\t\n147\tAll existing code patterns continue to work without modification:\n148\t\n149\t### Pattern 1: Direct struct creation\n150\t```go\n151\t// This continues to work\n152\tve := ValidationError{\n153\t    ErrorType: \"required\",\n154\t    Expected:  \"value\",\n155\t    Actual:    nil,\n156\t}\n157\t```\n158\t\n159\t### Pattern 2: Convenience functions\n160\t```go\n161\t// These continue to work\n162\terr := FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users\")\n163\terr := FormatErrorMessageError(\"invalid.*token\", \"access_denied\", \"error\", \"OAuth validation\")\n164\terr := FormatContentTypeError(\"application/json\", \"text/html\", \"API response\")\n165\t```\n166\t\n167\t### Pattern 3: Old-style positional parameters\n168\t```go\n169\t// This continues to work\n170\terr := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n171\t    \"status_code\", 200, 404, \"GET /api/users\", `{\"error\": \"not_found\"}`,\n172\t    \"\", \"\", nil, \"\", \"\", nil,\n173\t)\n174\t```\n175\t\n176\t## Performance Impact\n177\t\n178\tBenchmark results show that optional parameters have minimal performance impact:\n179\t\n180\t- **No optional parameters**: ~400 ns/op\n181\t- **All optional parameters**: ~390 ns/op\n182\t- **Performance difference**: Negligible (~2.5% faster with all parameters)\n183\t\n184\tThis demonstrates that the optional parameter implementation is efficient and does not degrade performance.\n185\t\n186\t## Testing Coverage\n187\t\n188\tComprehensive tests verify backward compatibility:\n189\t\n190\t1. **No optional parameter tests**: Verify formatters work with only required parameters\n191\t2. **Single optional parameter tests**: Verify each optional parameter works in isolation\n192\t3. **All optional parameters tests**: Verify all optional parameters work together\n193\t4. **Existing code compilation tests**: Verify old code patterns still compile\n194\t5. **Benchmark tests**: Verify performance hasn't degraded\n195\t\n196\tRun the backward compatibility test suite:\n197\t```bash\n198\tgo test ./internal/validate -run \"BackwardCompat|Optional\"\n199\t```\n200\t\n201\t## Migration Guide\n202\t\n203\t### When to add optional parameters\n204\t\n205\tAdd optional parameters when you need to:\n206\t- Provide more context about where the error occurred\n207\t- Explain pattern matching failures\n208\t- Show range validation boundaries\n209\t- Include detailed validation steps\n210\t- Override auto-generated suggestions with domain-specific guidance\n211\t\n212\t### When to keep minimal parameters\n213\t\n214\tKeep minimal parameters when:\n215\t- The error is self-explanatory\n216\t- Performance is critical (though impact is negligible)\n217\t- The additional context doesn't add value\n218\t- You're maintaining legacy code\n219\t\n220\t## Summary\n221\t\n222\tThe ARMOR error formatting system maintains full backward compatibility while providing rich optional fields for enhanced error messages. All existing code continues to work without modification, and new code can optionally use the enhanced features for more detailed error reporting.\n223\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"86d21a9e-32c5-4f35-b611-8c7940394de5","uuid":"6c1d10e0-e6a3-4bc1-a887-35c7ae2f502e","timestamp":"2026-07-15T20:10:52.161Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/validate/BACKWARD_COMPATIBILITY.md","content":"# Backward Compatibility of Optional Error Fields\n\n## Overview\n\nThis document describes the backward compatibility guarantees for the error formatting system in ARMOR. All parameters after the first 5 are optional:\n\n**Required Parameters:**\n- `validationType` string - The category of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\")\n- `expected` interface{} - The expected value\n- `actual` interface{} - The actual value received\n- `context` string - Additional context about the validation operation\n- `responseSnippet` string - Truncated response excerpt for debugging\n\n**Optional Parameters:**\n- `fieldName` string - The specific field name where the error was found (default: \"\")\n- `location` string - Location information (default: validation information (default: \"\")\n- `validationDetails` []string - Additional validation-specific details (default: nil)\n- `customSuggestions` ...string - Custom suggestions for fixing the error (default: auto-generated)\n\n**Example Usage:**\n\n```go\n// Minimal call with only required parameters\nerr := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n    \"status_code\",    // validationType\n    200,              // expected\n    404,              // actual\n    \"GET /api/users\", // context\n    `{\"error\": \"not_found\"}`, // responseSnippet\n    \"\",               // fieldName (optional, empty)\n    \"\",               // location (optional, empty)\n    nil,              // relatedFields (optional, nil)\n    \"\",               // patternDetails (optional, empty)\n    \"\",               // rangeInfo (optional, empty)\n    nil,              // validationDetails (optional, nil)\n)\n\n// Same call with optional parameters omitted for brevity\nerr := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n    \"status_code\", 200, 404, \"GET /api/users\", `{\"error\": \"not_found\"}`,\n    \"\", \"\", nil, \"\", \"\", nil,\n)\n\n// Full call with all optional parameters\nerr := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n    \"status_code\", 200, 404, \"GET /api/users\", `{\"error\": \"not_found\"}`,\n    \"response.status\",                        // fieldName\n    \"line 15 in api_client.go\",              // location\n    []string{\"error_code\", \"response_body\"}, // relatedFields\n    \"expected status code 2xx, got 404\",      // patternDetails\n    \"200-299 (Success)\",                     // rangeInfo\n    []string{\"Checked status code\", \"Expected 2xx\", \"Got 404\"}, // validationDetails\n    \"Verify the resource exists\", \"Check authentication\", \"Review API endpoint\", // customSuggestions\n)\n```\n\n### FormatValidationErrorFull\n\nThis function formats a ValidationError struct with optional context:\n\n**Parameters:**\n- `err` ValidationError - The validation error to format (required)\n- `includeSeverity` bool - Whether to include severity information (required)\n- `context` *ValidationErrorContext - Optional context information (optional, can be nil)\n\n**Example Usage:**\n\n```go\n// Without optional context\nerr := ValidationError{\n    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n    Message:   \"Field is required\",\n    FieldName: \"email\",\n}\nmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, nil)\n\n// With optional context\nctx := &ValidationErrorContext{\n    Location: \"line 5\",\n    RelatedFields: []string{\"email_confirmation\"},\n}\nmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, ctx)\n```\n\n### FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual\n\nThis function extends FormatValidationErrorFull with optional expected/actual value comparison:\n\n**Parameters:**\n- `err` ValidationError - The validation error to format (required)\n- `includeSeverity` bool - Whether to include severity information (required)\n- `context` *ValidationErrorContext - Optional context information (optional, can be nil)\n- `expectedActual` ExpectedActual - Optional expected/actual comparison (optional, can be empty)\n\n**Example Usage:**\n\n```go\n// Without optional parameters\nmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ExpectedActual{})\n\n// With optional expected/actual comparison\nea := ExpectedActual{Expected: 200, Actual: 404}\nmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ea)\n\n// With both context and expected/actual\nctx := &ValidationErrorContext{Location: \"line 20\"}\nea := ExpectedActual{Expected: []int{200, 201, 204}, Actual: 404}\nmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, ctx, ea)\n```\n\n## ValidationFormatter Builder\n\nThe `ValidationFormatter` provides a builder pattern for creating validation errors ergonomically:\n\n```go\n// Minimal usage - only required fields\nformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n    WithExpected(200).\n    WithActual(404).\n    Format()\n\n// Full usage - all optional fields\nformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n    WithExpected(200).\n    WithActual(404).\n    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n    WithResponseSnippet(`{\"error\": \"not_found\"}`).\n    WithFieldName(\"response.status\").\n    WithPatternDetails(\"expected status code 2xx, got 404\").\n    WithRangeInfo(\"200-299 (Success)\").\n    WithValidationDetails(\"Checked status code\", \"Expected 2xx\", \"Got 404\").\n    WithSuggestions(\"Verify the resource exists\", \"Check authentication\").\n    Format()\n```\n\n## Backward Compatibility Verification\n\nAll existing code patterns continue to work without modification:\n\n### Pattern 1: Direct struct creation\n```go\n// This continues to work\nve := ValidationError{\n    ErrorType: \"required\",\n    Expected:  \"value\",\n    Actual:    nil,\n}\n```\n\n### Pattern 2: Convenience functions\n```go\n// These continue to work\nerr := FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users\")\nerr := FormatErrorMessageError(\"invalid.*token\", \"access_denied\", \"error\", \"OAuth validation\")\nerr := FormatContentTypeError(\"application/json\", \"text/html\", \"API response\")\n```\n\n### Pattern 3: Old-style positional parameters\n```go\n// This continues to work\nerr := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n    \"status_code\", 200, 404, \"GET /api/users\", `{\"error\": \"not_found\"}`,\n    \"\", \"\", nil, \"\", \"\", nil,\n)\n```\n\n## Performance Impact\n\nBenchmark results show that optional parameters have minimal performance impact:\n\n- **No optional parameters**: ~400 ns/op\n- **All optional parameters**: ~390 ns/op\n- **Performance difference**: Negligible (~2.5% faster with all parameters)\n\nThis demonstrates that the optional parameter implementation is efficient and does not degrade performance.\n\n## Testing Coverage\n\nComprehensive tests verify backward compatibility:\n\n1. **No optional parameter tests**: Verify formatters work with only required parameters\n2. **Single optional parameter tests**: Verify each optional parameter works in isolation\n3. **All optional parameters tests**: Verify all optional parameters work together\n4. **Existing code compilation tests**: Verify old code patterns still compile\n5. **Benchmark tests**: Verify performance hasn't degraded\n\nRun the backward compatibility test suite:\n```bash\ngo test ./internal/validate -run \"BackwardCompat|Optional\"\n```\n\n## Migration Guide\n\n### When to add optional parameters\n\nAdd optional parameters when you need to:\n- Provide more context about where the error occurred\n- Explain pattern matching failures\n- Show range validation boundaries\n- Include detailed validation steps\n- Override auto-generated suggestions with domain-specific guidance\n\n### When to keep minimal parameters\n\nKeep minimal parameters when:\n- The error is self-explanatory\n- Performance is critical (though impact is negligible)\n- The additional context doesn't add value\n- You're maintaining legacy code\n\n## Summary\n\nThe ARMOR error formatting system maintains full backward compatibility while providing rich optional fields for enhanced error messages. All existing code continues to work without modification, and new code can optionally use the enhanced features for more detailed error reporting.\n","numLines":223,"startLine":1,"totalLines":223}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_a8bf471937be4832b16b9416","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Validation Package Types and Relationships\n2\t\n3\tThis document provides comprehensive documentation of all types in the validation package and their relationships.\n4\t\n5\t## Overview\n6\t\n7\tThe validation package provides a comprehensive system for HTTP API validation with standardized error reporting. The types are organized into several categories:\n8\t\n9\t- **Core Error Types**: `ValidationError` and related constants\n10\t- **Error Categorization**: `ErrorCategory`, `ValidationErrorType`\n11\t- **Helper Types**: Configuration and result types for specific validation scenarios\n12\t- **Builder Types**: Fluent API for constructing validation errors\n13\t\n14\t## Core Error Type: ValidationError\n15\t\n16\t### Purpose\n17\t\n18\t`ValidationError` is the primary data structure for representing validation failures. It provides a standardized, machine-readable format for validation errors across different types of API validations.\n19\t\n20\t### Structure\n21\t\n22\t```go\n23\ttype ValidationError struct {\n24\t    // Required fields\n25\t    ErrorType string  // The validation category (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\")\n26\t    Message   string  // Human-readable description of the validation failure\n27\t\n28\t    // Optional fields for additional context\n29\t    Context           string        // Where/when the validation occurred\n30\t    Expected          interface{}   // The expected value\n31\t    Actual            interface{}   // The actual value received\n32\t    FieldName         string        // Specific field where the error was found\n33\t    Location          string        // Position information\n34\t    RelatedFields     []string      // Related fields for additional context\n35\t    PatternDetails    string        // Pattern matching failure information\n36\t    RangeInfo         string        // Range boundary information\n37\t    ValidationDetails []string      // Additional validation-specific information\n38\t    ResponseSnippet   string        // Truncated response excerpt\n39\t    Suggestions       []string      // Actionable recommendations\n40\t}\n41\t```\n42\t\n43\t### Key Relationships\n44\t\n45\t1. **With ErrorType Constants**: Uses constants from `error_categories.go`\n46\t2. **With ValidationFormatter**: Built using the builder pattern\n47\t3. **With FormatOption**: Configured using functional options\n48\t4. **With ValidationErrors**: Can be serialized to maps and JSON\n49\t\n50\t### Usage Example\n51\t\n52\t```go\n53\t// Direct construction\n54\terr := ValidationError{\n55\t    ErrorType: ErrorTypeStatusCode,\n56\t    Message:   \"Expected status code 200 but got 404\",\n57\t    Expected:  200,\n58\t    Actual:    404,\n59\t    Context:   \"GET /api/users/123\",\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\t// Using the builder pattern\n63\terr := NewValidationFormatter(ErrorTypeStatusCode).\n64\t    WithExpected(200).\n65\t    WithActual(404).\n66\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n67\t    Format()\n68\t```\n69\t\n70\t## Error Categorization Types\n71\t\n72\t### ErrorCategory\n73\t\n74\t`ErrorCategory` is a high-level categorization type that groups related error types:\n75\t\n76\t```go\n77\ttype ErrorCategory string\n78\t\n79\tconst (\n80\t    CategoryHTTP        ErrorCategory = \"http\"\n81\t    CategoryContent     ErrorCategory = \"content\"\n82\t    CategoryValidation  ErrorCategory = \"validation\"\n83\t    CategoryPerformance ErrorCategory = \"performance\"\n84\t    CategorySecurity    ErrorCategory = \"security\"\n85\t    CategoryCustom      ErrorCategory = \"custom\"\n86\t)\n87\t```\n88\t\n89\t### ValidationErrorType\n90\t\n91\t`ValidationErrorType` is a strongly-typed enum for validation error categories:\n92\t\n93\t```go\n94\ttype ValidationErrorType string\n95\t\n96\tconst (\n97\t    TypeStatusCode          ValidationErrorType = \"status_code\"\n98\t    TypeStatusCodeRange     ValidationErrorType = \"status_code_range\"\n99\t    TypeContentType         ValidationErrorType = \"content_type\"\n100\t    TypeErrorMessage        ValidationErrorType = \"error_message\"\n101\t    // ... and more\n102\t)\n103\t```\n104\t\n105\t### Type Relationships\n106\t\n107\t```\n108\tValidationErrorType (enum)\n109\t    ↓ .String() → string\n110\t    ↓ .Category() → ErrorCategory\n111\t    ↓ .Description() → string\n112\t\n113\tErrorCategory (enum)\n114\t    ↓ String() → string\n115\t    ↓ GetErrorTypesInCategory() → []string\n116\t\n117\tValidationError (struct)\n118\t    ↓ ErrorType field → string (should use ValidationErrorType.String())\n119\t    ↓ Validate() → error\n120\t    ↓ ToMap() → map[string]interface{}\n121\t```\n122\t\n123\t## Helper Types\n124\t\n125\t### ValidationFormatter (Builder Pattern)\n126\t\n127\t`ValidationFormatter` provides a fluent API for constructing `ValidationError` instances:\n128\t\n129\t```go\n130\ttype ValidationFormatter struct {\n131\t    validationType     string\n132\t    expected          interface{}\n133\t    actual            interface{}\n134\t    context           string\n135\t    responseSnippet   string\n136\t    fieldName         string\n137\t    patternDetails    string\n138\t    rangeInfo         string\n139\t    validationDetails []string\n140\t    customSuggestions []string\n141\t}\n142\t```\n143\t\n144\t**Relationships**:\n145\t- **Creates**: `ValidationError` instances\n146\t- **Uses**: Error type constants for validation types\n147\t- **Configures**: All ValidationError fields through builder methods\n148\t\n149\t**Usage Example**:\n150\t```go\n151\terr := NewValidationFormatter(ErrorTypeStatusCode).\n152\t    WithExpected(200).\n153\t    WithActual(404).\n154\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n155\t    WithSuggestions(\"Check endpoint URL\").\n156\t    Format()\n157\t```\n158\t\n159\t### FormatOption (Functional Options Pattern)\n160\t\n161\t`FormatOption` provides functional configuration for `FormatCustomValidationError`:\n162\t\n163\t```go\n164\ttype FormatOption func(*FormatConfig)\n165\t\n166\ttype FormatConfig struct {\n167\t    Context            string\n168\t    ResponseSnippet    string\n169\t    FieldName          string\n170\t    PatternDetails     string\n171\t    RangeInfo          string\n172\t    ValidationDetails  []string\n173\t    Suggestions        []string\n174\t}\n175\t```\n176\t\n177\t**Relationships**:\n178\t- **Configures**: `FormatCustomValidationError` function\n179\t- **Modifies**: `FormatConfig` struct\n180\t- **Creates**: Custom `ValidationError` instances\n181\t\n182\t**Usage Example**:\n183\t```go\n184\terr := FormatCustomValidationError(\n185\t    \"custom_field\",\n186\t    \"expected_value\",\n187\t    \"actual_value\",\n188\t    WithContext(\"custom validation\"),\n189\t    WithFieldName(\"field.name\"),\n190\t    WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\"),\n191\t)\n192\t```\n193\t\n194\t### CORSConfig (CORS Validation)\n195\t\n196\t`CORSConfig` specifies expected CORS header values for validation:\n197\t\n198\t```go\n199\ttype CORSConfig struct {\n200\t    AllowOrigin     string\n201\t    AllowMethods    string\n202\t    AllowHeaders    string\n203\t    AllowCredentials bool\n204\t    ExposeHeaders   string\n205\t    MaxAge          string\n206\t}\n207\t```\n208\t\n209\t**Relationships**:\n210\t- **Validates**: HTTP response headers\n211\t- **Used by**: `CORSHeadersIsValid` function\n212\t- **Related to**: `ValidationError` with `ErrorTypeCORSHeaders`\n213\t\n214\t### ErrorResponseFieldNames (Field Name Configuration)\n215\t\n216\t`ErrorResponseFieldNames` specifies custom field names for error response validation:\n217\t\n218\t```go\n219\ttype ErrorResponseFieldNames struct {\n220\t    PrimaryFieldName   string\n221\t    SecondaryFieldName string\n222\t}\n223\t```\n224\t\n225\t**Relationships**:\n226\t- **Configures**: Which fields to check in error responses\n227\t- **Used by**: `ErrorResponseStructureIsValid` function\n228\t- **Related to**: `ValidationError` with `FieldName` field\n229\t\n230\t### ErrorMessagePattern (Pattern Matching)\n231\t\n232\t`ErrorMessagePattern` defines pattern matching configuration for error messages:\n233\t\n234\t```go\n235\ttype ErrorMessagePattern struct {\n236\t    Pattern         string\n237\t    CaseInsensitive bool\n238\t    MatchAny        bool\n239\t    FieldNames      []string\n240\t}\n241\t```\n242\t\n243\t**Relationships**:\n244\t- **Validates**: Error message content against regex patterns\n245\t- **Used by**: `ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig` function\n246\t- **Related to**: `ValidationError` with `PatternDetails` field\n247\t\n248\t## Validation Result Types\n249\t\n250\t### StatusCodeValidationResult\n251\t\n252\t`StatusCodeValidationResult` provides detailed information about status code validation:\n253\t\n254\t```go\n255\ttype StatusCodeValidationResult struct {\n256\t    Valid            bool\n257\t    ActualCode       int\n258\t    ExpectedCodes    []int\n259\t    MatchedCode      *int\n260\t    MismatchDetails  string\n261\t    IsClientError    bool\n262\t    IsServerError    bool\n263\t    Category         string\n264\t}\n265\t```\n266\t\n267\t**Relationships**:\n268\t- **Returned by**: `ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails` function\n269\t- **Provides**: More detailed information than boolean functions\n270\t- **Related to**: `ValidationError` with `ErrorTypeStatusCode`\n271\t\n272\t### ErrorMessageValidationResult\n273\t\n274\t`ErrorMessageValidationResult` provides detailed information about error message validation:\n275\t\n276\t```go\n277\ttype ErrorMessageValidationResult struct {\n278\t    Valid                 bool\n279\t    Found                 bool\n280\t    Message               string\n281\t    FieldName             string\n282\t    PatternMatched        bool\n283\t    MustContainResults    map[string]bool\n284\t    MustNotContainResults map[string]bool\n285\t    LengthValidation      bool\n286\t    Issues                []string\n287\t}\n288\t```\n289\t\n290\t**Relationships**:\n291\t- **Returned by**: `ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails` function\n292\t- **Provides**: Comprehensive validation results\n293\t- **Related to**: `ValidationError` with `ErrorTypeErrorMessage`\n294\t\n295\t## Range and Pattern Types\n296\t\n297\t### StatusCodeRange\n298\t\n299\t`StatusCodeRange` defines a range of status codes for flexible validation:\n300\t\n301\t```go\n302\ttype StatusCodeRange struct {\n303\t    Min         int\n304\t    Max         int\n305\t    Description string\n306\t}\n307\t```\n308\t\n309\t**Relationships**:\n310\t- **Used by**: `ValidateStatusCodeRange` function\n311\t- **Related to**: `ValidationError` with `ErrorTypeStatusCodeRange` and `RangeInfo` field\n312\t\n313\t**Common Ranges**:\n314\t```go\n315\tvar (\n316\t    Range1xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 100, Max: 199, Description: \"Informational\"}\n317\t    Range2xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 200, Max: 299, Description: \"Success\"}\n318\t    Range3xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 300, Max: 399, Description: \"Redirection\"}\n319\t    Range4xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 400, Max: 499, Description: \"Client Error\"}\n320\t    Range5xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 500, Max: 599, Description: \"Server Error\"}\n321\t)\n322\t```\n323\t\n324\t### EnhancedErrorMessagePattern\n325\t\n326\t`EnhancedErrorMessagePattern` defines advanced pattern matching for error messages:\n327\t\n328\t```go\n329\ttype EnhancedErrorMessagePattern struct {\n330\t    FieldNames      *ErrorResponseFieldNames\n331\t    Pattern         string\n332\t    CaseInsensitive bool\n333\t    MustContain    []string\n334\t    MustNotContain []string\n335\t    MinLength       int\n336\t    MaxLength       int\n337\t}\n338\t```\n339\t\n340\t**Relationships**:\n341\t- **Extends**: Basic `ErrorMessagePattern` with more validation options\n342\t- **Used by**: `ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails` function\n343\t- **Related to**: `ValidationError` with multiple validation detail fields\n344\t\n345\t## Error Type Collections\n346\t\n347\t### ErrorTypeGroup\n348\t\n349\t`ErrorTypeGroup` represents a collection of related error types for bulk operations:\n350\t\n351\t```go\n352\ttype ErrorTypeGroup []string\n353\t```\n354\t\n355\t**Predefined Groups**:\n356\t```go\n357\tvar (\n358\t    HTTPErrorTypes        ErrorTypeGroup  // All HTTP-related types\n359\t    ContentErrorTypes     ErrorTypeGroup  // All content-related types\n360\t    ValidationErrorTypes  ErrorTypeGroup  // All validation-related types\n361\t    PerformanceErrorTypes ErrorTypeGroup  // All performance-related types\n362\t    StatusCodeErrorTypes  ErrorTypeGroup  // Status code types\n363\t    MessageErrorTypes     ErrorTypeGroup  // Error message types\n364\t    HeaderErrorTypes      ErrorTypeGroup  // Header validation types\n365\t)\n366\t```\n367\t\n368\t**Relationships**:\n369\t- **Groups**: Related error type constants\n370\t- **Provides**: Convenient access to error type categories\n371\t- **Used by**: Error filtering and categorization\n372\t\n373\t## Type Conversion Flow\n374\t\n375\t### String to ValidationErrorType\n376\t\n377\t```go\n378\t// Input: string\n379\terrorTypeStr := \"status_code\"\n380\t\n381\t// Convert to ValidationErrorType\n382\terrorType := ValidationErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n383\t\n384\t// Get string representation\n385\terrorType.String() // \"status_code\"\n386\t\n387\t// Get category\n388\terrorType.Category() // CategoryHTTP\n389\t\n390\t// Get description\n391\terrorType.Description() // \"HTTP status code validation\"\n392\t\n393\t// Use in ValidationError\n394\tve := ValidationError{\n395\t    ErrorType: errorType.String(),\n396\t    Message:   \"Validation failed\",\n397\t}\n398\t```\n399\t\n400\t### ValidationError to Map/JSON\n401\t\n402\t```go\n403\t// Create ValidationError\n404\tve := ValidationError{\n405\t    ErrorType: ErrorTypeStatusCode,\n406\t    Message:   \"Expected 200 but got 404\",\n407\t    Expected:  200,\n408\t    Actual:    404,\n409\t}\n410\t\n411\t// Convert to map\n412\tdata := ve.ToMap()\n413\t// map[string]interface{}{\n414\t//     \"error_type\": \"status_code\",\n415\t//     \"message\": \"Expected 200 but got 404\",\n416\t//     \"expected\": 200,\n417\t//     \"actual\": 404,\n418\t// }\n419\t\n420\t// Convert to JSON\n421\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(ve)\n422\t// {\"error_type\":\"status_code\",\"message\":\"Expected 200 but got 404\",\"expected\":200,\"actual\":404}\n423\t```\n424\t\n425\t### ValidationFormatter to ValidationError\n426\t\n427\t```go\n428\t// Create ValidationFormatter\n429\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(ErrorTypeStatusCode).\n430\t    WithExpected(200).\n431\t    WithActual(404).\n432\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\")\n433\t\n434\t// Build ValidationError\n435\tve := formatter.Format()\n436\t\n437\t// Result is a ValidationError with all configured fields\n438\t```\n439\t\n440\t## Error Type Validation\n441\t\n442\t### Validation Chain\n443\t\n444\t```go\n445\t// 1. Create error type from string\n446\terrorType := ValidationErrorTypeFromString(\"status_code\")\n447\t\n448\t// 2. Validate the error type\n449\tif err := errorType.Validate(); err != nil {\n450\t    log.Printf(\"Invalid error type: %v\", err)\n451\t}\n452\t\n453\t// 3. Get category information\n454\tcategory := errorType.Category()\n455\t\n456\t// 4. Use in ValidationError\n457\tve := ValidationError{\n458\t    ErrorType: errorType.String(),\n459\t    Message:   \"Validation failed\",\n460\t}\n461\t\n462\t// 5. Validate the ValidationError\n463\tif err := ve.Validate(); err != nil {\n464\t    log.Printf(\"Invalid ValidationError: %v\", err)\n465\t}\n466\t```\n467\t\n468\t### Type Safety Patterns\n469\t\n470\t```go\n471\t// Pattern 1: Use constants directly\n472\tve := ValidationError{\n473\t    ErrorType: ErrorTypeStatusCode,  // Type-safe constant\n474\t    Message:   \"Failed\",\n475\t}\n476\t\n477\t// Pattern 2: Use enum type\n478\terrorType := TypeStatusCode\n479\tve := ValidationError{\n480\t    ErrorType: errorType.String(),  // Convert enum to string\n481\t    Message:   \"Failed\",\n482\t}\n483\t\n484\t// Pattern 3: Validate user input\n485\terrorTypeStr := getUserInput()\n486\tif !IsValidErrorType(errorTypeStr) {\n487\t    return fmt.Errorf(\"invalid error type: %s\", errorTypeStr)\n488\t}\n489\tve := ValidationError{\n490\t    ErrorType: errorTypeStr,\n491\t    Message:   \"Failed\",\n492\t}\n493\t```\n494\t\n495\t## Integration Examples\n496\t\n497\t### Example 1: HTTP Status Code Validation\n498\t\n499\t```go\n500\t// Validate status code\n501\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(resp, 200)\n502\t\n503\tif !result.Valid {\n504\t    // Create ValidationError using the builder\n505\t    err := NewValidationFormatter(ErrorTypeStatusCode).\n506\t        WithExpected(200).\n507\t        WithActual(result.ActualCode).\n508\t        WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n509\t        Format()\n510\t\n511\t    // Log the error\n512\t    log.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\n513\t\n514\t    // Or convert to JSON for API response\n515\t    jsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(err.ToMap())\n516\t}\n517\t```\n518\t\n519\t### Example 2: Error Message Pattern Validation\n520\t\n521\t```go\n522\t// Validate error message pattern\n523\tpattern := EnhancedErrorMessagePattern{\n524\t    FieldNames: DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames(),\n525\t    Pattern:    \"invalid.*token\",\n526\t    MustContain: []string{\"expired\", \"invalid\"},\n527\t}\n528\t\n529\tresult := ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails(responseBody, pattern)\n530\t\n531\tif !result.Valid {\n532\t    // Create ValidationError with details\n533\t    err := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n534\t        ErrorTypeErrorMessagePattern,\n535\t        \"invalid.*token\",\n536\t        result.Message,\n537\t        \"OAuth token validation\",\n538\t        extractResponseSnippet(responseBody),\n539\t        result.FieldName,\n540\t        \"\",\n541\t        nil,\n542\t        \"Pattern did not match\",\n543\t        \"\",\n544\t        result.Issues,\n545\t    )\n546\t\n547\t    log.Printf(\"Message validation failed: %v\", err)\n548\t}\n549\t```\n550\t\n551\t### Example 3: CORS Header Validation\n552\t\n553\t```go\n554\t// Define expected CORS configuration\n555\tconfig := &CORSConfig{\n556\t    AllowOrigin:     \"https://example.com\",\n557\t    AllowMethods:    \"GET, POST, OPTIONS\",\n558\t    AllowHeaders:    \"Content-Type, Authorization\",\n559\t    AllowCredentials: true,\n560\t}\n561\t\n562\t// Validate CORS headers\n563\tif !CORSHeadersIsValid(resp, config) {\n564\t    // Create ValidationError\n565\t    err := FormatValidationError(\n566\t        ErrorTypeCORSHeaders,\n567\t        \"CORS headers configured\",\n568\t        \"CORS headers missing or invalid\",\n569\t        \"GET /api/data\",\n570\t        \"\",\n571\t    )\n572\t\n573\t    log.Printf(\"CORS validation failed: %v\", err)\n574\t}\n575\t```\n576\t\n577\t## Best Practices\n578\t\n579\t### 1. Use Constants for Error Types\n580\t\n581\t❌ **Avoid**: Magic strings\n582\t```go\n583\tve := ValidationError{\n584\t    ErrorType: \"status_code\",  // Typo-prone\n585\t    Message:   \"Failed\",\n586\t}\n587\t```\n588\t\n589\t✅ **Prefer**: Type-safe constants\n590\t```go\n591\tve := ValidationError{\n592\t    ErrorType: ErrorTypeStatusCode,  // Type-safe\n593\t    Message:   \"Failed\",\n594\t}\n595\t```\n596\t\n597\t### 2. Validate Error Types\n598\t\n599\t❌ **Avoid**: Assuming input is valid\n600\t```go\n601\tfunc CreateError(errorType string) ValidationError {\n602\t    return ValidationError{ErrorType: errorType}  // No validation\n603\t}\n604\t```\n605\t\n606\t✅ **Prefer**: Validate before use\n607\t```go\n608\tfunc CreateError(errorType string) (ValidationError, error) {\n609\t    if err := ValidateErrorType(errorType); err != nil {\n610\t        return ValidationError{}, err\n611\t    }\n612\t    return ValidationError{ErrorType: errorType}, nil\n613\t}\n614\t```\n615\t\n616\t### 3. Use Builder Pattern for Complex Errors\n617\t\n618\t❌ **Avoid**: Direct construction with many fields\n619\t```go\n620\tve := ValidationError{\n621\t    ErrorType: \"status_code\",\n622\t    Message:   \"Failed\",\n623\t    Expected:  200,\n624\t    Actual:    404,\n625\t    Context:   \"GET /api/users\",\n626\t    FieldName: \"\",\n627\t    Location:  \"\",\n628\t    // ... many more fields\n629\t}\n630\t```\n631\t\n632\t✅ **Prefer**: Builder pattern for readability\n633\t```go\n634\tve := NewValidationFormatter(ErrorTypeStatusCode).\n635\t    WithExpected(200).\n636\t    WithActual(404).\n637\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n638\t    Format()\n639\t```\n640\t\n641\t### 4. Leverage Type Categories\n642\t\n643\t❌ **Avoid**: Manual category checking\n644\t```go\n645\tif errorType == \"status_code\" || errorType == \"content_type\" || errorType == \"cors_headers\" {\n646\t    // Handle HTTP errors\n647\t}\n648\t```\n649\t\n650\t✅ **Prefer**: Category-based logic\n651\t```go\n652\tif IsHTTPErrorType(errorType) {\n653\t    // Handle HTTP errors\n654\t}\n655\t```\n656\t\n657\t## Type Relationship Diagram\n658\t\n659\t```\n660\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n661\t│                    ValidationError (Core)                    │\n662\t│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\n663\t│  │ ErrorType (string) ←── ErrorType constants          │   │\n664\t│  │ Message   (string) fields ...                             │   │\n668\t│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n669\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n670\t                           │\n671\t                           │ uses\n672\t                           ↓\n673\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n674\t│              ErrorType Constants & Enums                     │\n675\t│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\n676\t│  │ ErrorTypeStatusCode, ErrorTypeErrorMessage, ...     │   │\n677\t│  │ (string constants from error_categories.go)          │   │\n678\t│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n679\t│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\n680\t│  │ TypeStatusCode, TypeErrorMessage, ...               │   │\n681\t│  │ (ValidationErrorType enum from error_type_enum.go)  │   │\n682\t│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n683\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n684\t                           │\n685\t                           │ categorizes\n686\t                           ↓\n687\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n688\t│                    ErrorCategory (Enum)                      │\n689\t│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\n690\t│  │ CategoryHTTP, CategoryContent, CategoryValidation, │   │\n691\t│  │ CategoryPerformance, CategorySecurity, CategoryCustom│   │\n692\t│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n693\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n694\t                           │\n695\t                           │ creates\n696\t                           ↓\n697\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n698\t│              Builder & Helper Types                         │\n699\t│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\n700\t│  │ ValidationFormatter (builder pattern)                │   │\n701\t│  │ FormatOption (functional options)                    │   │\n702\t│  │ CORSConfig, ErrorResponseFieldNames, ...             │   │\n703\t│  │ StatusCodeValidationResult, ErrorMessagePattern, ...│   │\n704\t│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n705\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n706\t                           │\n707\t                           │ organizes\n708\t                           ↓\n709\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n710\t│                 Error Type Collections                       │\n711\t│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\n712\t│  │ ErrorTypeGroup, ValidationErrorTypes                 │   │\n713\t│  │ HTTPErrorTypes, ContentErrorTypes, ...              │   │\n714\t│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n715\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n716\t```\n717\t\n718\t## Summary\n719\t\n720\tThe validation package provides a comprehensive type system for HTTP API validation:\n721\t\n722\t1. **ValidationError** is the core error representation\n723\t2. **ErrorCategory** groups related error types\n724\t3. **ValidationErrorType** provides strongly-typed error constants\n725\t4. **Helper types** enable flexible validation scenarios\n726\t5. **Builder types** provide ergonomic construction APIs\n727\t6. **Result types** provide detailed validation information\n728\t\n729\tAll types work together to provide type-safe, flexible, and comprehensive validation error reporting for HTTP API testing and Validation Package Types and Relationships\n\nThis document provides comprehensive documentation of all types in the validation package and their relationships.\n\n## Overview\n\nThe validation package provides a comprehensive system for HTTP API validation with standardized error reporting. The types are organized into several categories:\n\n- **Core Error Types**: `ValidationError` and related constants\n- **Error Categorization**: `ErrorCategory`, `ValidationErrorType`\n- **Helper Types**: Configuration and result types for specific validation scenarios\n- **Builder Types**: Fluent API for constructing validation errors\n\n## Core Error Type: ValidationError\n\n### Purpose\n\n`ValidationError` is the primary data structure for representing validation failures. It provides a standardized, machine-readable format for validation errors across different types of API validations.\n\n### Structure\n\n```go\ntype ValidationError struct {\n    // Required fields\n    ErrorType string  // The validation category (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\")\n    Message   string  // Human-readable description of the validation failure\n\n    // Optional fields for additional context\n    Context           string        // Where/when the validation occurred\n    Expected          interface{}   // The expected value\n    Actual            interface{}   // The actual value received\n    FieldName         string        // Specific field where the error was found\n    Location          string        // Position information\n    RelatedFields     []string      // Related fields for additional context\n    PatternDetails    string        // Pattern matching failure information\n    RangeInfo         string        // Range boundary information\n    ValidationDetails []string      // Additional validation-specific information\n    ResponseSnippet   string        // Truncated response excerpt\n    Suggestions       []string      // Actionable recommendations\n}\n```\n\n### Key Relationships\n\n1. **With ErrorType Constants**: Uses constants from `error_categories.go`\n2. **With ValidationFormatter**: Built using the builder pattern\n3. **With FormatOption**: Configured using functional options\n4. **With ValidationErrors**: Can be serialized to maps and JSON\n\n### Usage Example\n\n```go\n// Direct construction\nerr := ValidationError{\n    ErrorType: ErrorTypeStatusCode,\n    Message:   \"Expected status code 200 but got 404\",\n    Expected:  200,\n    Actual:    404,\n    Context:   \"GET /api/users/123\",\n}\n\n// Using the builder pattern\nerr := NewValidationFormatter(ErrorTypeStatusCode).\n    WithExpected(200).\n    WithActual(404).\n    WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n    Format()\n```\n\n## Error Categorization Types\n\n### ErrorCategory\n\n`ErrorCategory` is a high-level categorization type that groups related error types:\n\n```go\ntype ErrorCategory string\n\nconst (\n    CategoryHTTP        ErrorCategory = \"http\"\n    CategoryContent     ErrorCategory = \"content\"\n    CategoryValidation  ErrorCategory = \"validation\"\n    CategoryPerformance ErrorCategory = \"performance\"\n    CategorySecurity    ErrorCategory = \"security\"\n    CategoryCustom      ErrorCategory = \"custom\"\n)\n```\n\n### ValidationErrorType\n\n`ValidationErrorType` is a strongly-typed enum for validation error categories:\n\n```go\ntype ValidationErrorType string\n\nconst (\n    TypeStatusCode          ValidationErrorType = \"status_code\"\n    TypeStatusCodeRange     ValidationErrorType = \"status_code_range\"\n    TypeContentType         ValidationErrorType = \"content_type\"\n    TypeErrorMessage        ValidationErrorType = \"error_message\"\n    // ... and more\n)\n```\n\n### Type Relationships\n\n```\nValidationErrorType (enum)\n    ↓ .String() → string\n    ↓ .Category() → ErrorCategory\n    ↓ .Description() → string\n\nErrorCategory (enum)\n    ↓ String() → string\n    ↓ GetErrorTypesInCategory() → []string\n\nValidationError (struct)\n    ↓ ErrorType field → string (should use ValidationErrorType.String())\n    ↓ Validate() → error\n    ↓ ToMap() → map[string]interface{}\n```\n\n## Helper Types\n\n### ValidationFormatter (Builder Pattern)\n\n`ValidationFormatter` provides a fluent API for constructing `ValidationError` instances:\n\n```go\ntype ValidationFormatter struct {\n    validationType     string\n    expected          interface{}\n    actual            interface{}\n    context           string\n    responseSnippet   string\n    fieldName         string\n    patternDetails    string\n    rangeInfo         string\n    validationDetails []string\n    customSuggestions []string\n}\n```\n\n**Relationships**:\n- **Creates**: `ValidationError` instances\n- **Uses**: Error type constants for validation types\n- **Configures**: All ValidationError fields through builder methods\n\n**Usage Example**:\n```go\nerr := NewValidationFormatter(ErrorTypeStatusCode).\n    WithExpected(200).\n    WithActual(404).\n    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n    WithSuggestions(\"Check endpoint URL\").\n    Format()\n```\n\n### FormatOption (Functional Options Pattern)\n\n`FormatOption` provides functional configuration for `FormatCustomValidationError`:\n\n```go\ntype FormatOption func(*FormatConfig)\n\ntype FormatConfig struct {\n    Context            string\n    ResponseSnippet    string\n    FieldName          string\n    PatternDetails     string\n    RangeInfo          string\n    ValidationDetails  []string\n    Suggestions        []string\n}\n```\n\n**Relationships**:\n- **Configures**: `FormatCustomValidationError` function\n- **Modifies**: `FormatConfig` struct\n- **Creates**: Custom `ValidationError` instances\n\n**Usage Example**:\n```go\nerr := FormatCustomValidationError(\n    \"custom_field\",\n    \"expected_value\",\n    \"actual_value\",\n    WithContext(\"custom validation\"),\n    WithFieldName(\"field.name\"),\n    WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\"),\n)\n```\n\n### CORSConfig (CORS Validation)\n\n`CORSConfig` specifies expected CORS header values for validation:\n\n```go\ntype CORSConfig struct {\n    AllowOrigin     string\n    AllowMethods    string\n    AllowHeaders    string\n    AllowCredentials bool\n    ExposeHeaders   string\n    MaxAge          string\n}\n```\n\n**Relationships**:\n- **Validates**: HTTP response headers\n- **Used by**: `CORSHeadersIsValid` function\n- **Related to**: `ValidationError` with `ErrorTypeCORSHeaders`\n\n### ErrorResponseFieldNames (Field Name Configuration)\n\n`ErrorResponseFieldNames` specifies custom field names for error response validation:\n\n```go\ntype ErrorResponseFieldNames struct {\n    PrimaryFieldName   string\n    SecondaryFieldName string\n}\n```\n\n**Relationships**:\n- **Configures**: Which fields to check in error responses\n- **Used by**: `ErrorResponseStructureIsValid` function\n- **Related to**: `ValidationError` with `FieldName` field\n\n### ErrorMessagePattern (Pattern Matching)\n\n`ErrorMessagePattern` defines pattern matching configuration for error messages:\n\n```go\ntype ErrorMessagePattern struct {\n    Pattern         string\n    CaseInsensitive bool\n    MatchAny        bool\n    FieldNames      []string\n}\n```\n\n**Relationships**:\n- **Validates**: Error message content against regex patterns\n- **Used by**: `ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig` function\n- **Related to**: `ValidationError` with `PatternDetails` field\n\n## Validation Result Types\n\n### StatusCodeValidationResult\n\n`StatusCodeValidationResult` provides detailed information about status code validation:\n\n```go\ntype StatusCodeValidationResult struct {\n    Valid            bool\n    ActualCode       int\n    ExpectedCodes    []int\n    MatchedCode      *int\n    MismatchDetails  string\n    IsClientError    bool\n    IsServerError    bool\n    Category         string\n}\n```\n\n**Relationships**:\n- **Returned by**: `ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails` function\n- **Provides**: More detailed information than boolean functions\n- **Related to**: `ValidationError` with `ErrorTypeStatusCode`\n\n### ErrorMessageValidationResult\n\n`ErrorMessageValidationResult` provides detailed information about error message validation:\n\n```go\ntype ErrorMessageValidationResult struct {\n    Valid                 bool\n    Found                 bool\n    Message               string\n    FieldName             string\n    PatternMatched        bool\n    MustContainResults    map[string]bool\n    MustNotContainResults map[string]bool\n    LengthValidation      bool\n    Issues                []string\n}\n```\n\n**Relationships**:\n- **Returned by**: `ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails` function\n- **Provides**: Comprehensive validation results\n- **Related to**: `ValidationError` with `ErrorTypeErrorMessage`\n\n## Range and Pattern Types\n\n### StatusCodeRange\n\n`StatusCodeRange` defines a range of status codes for flexible validation:\n\n```go\ntype StatusCodeRange struct {\n    Min         int\n    Max         int\n    Description string\n}\n```\n\n**Relationships**:\n- **Used by**: `ValidateStatusCodeRange` function\n- **Related to**: `ValidationError` with `ErrorTypeStatusCodeRange` and `RangeInfo` field\n\n**Common Ranges**:\n```go\nvar (\n    Range1xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 100, Max: 199, Description: \"Informational\"}\n    Range2xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 200, Max: 299, Description: \"Success\"}\n    Range3xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 300, Max: 399, Description: \"Redirection\"}\n    Range4xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 400, Max: 499, Description: \"Client Error\"}\n    Range5xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 500, Max: 599, Description: \"Server Error\"}\n)\n```\n\n### EnhancedErrorMessagePattern\n\n`EnhancedErrorMessagePattern` defines advanced pattern matching for error messages:\n\n```go\ntype EnhancedErrorMessagePattern struct {\n    FieldNames      *ErrorResponseFieldNames\n    Pattern         string\n    CaseInsensitive bool\n    MustContain    []string\n    MustNotContain []string\n    MinLength       int\n    MaxLength       int\n}\n```\n\n**Relationships**:\n- **Extends**: Basic `ErrorMessagePattern` with more validation options\n- **Used by**: `ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails` function\n- **Related to**: `ValidationError` with multiple validation detail fields\n\n## Error Type Collections\n\n### ErrorTypeGroup\n\n`ErrorTypeGroup` represents a collection of related error types for bulk operations:\n\n```go\ntype ErrorTypeGroup []string\n```\n\n**Predefined Groups**:\n```go\nvar (\n    HTTPErrorTypes        ErrorTypeGroup  // All HTTP-related types\n    ContentErrorTypes     ErrorTypeGroup  // All content-related types\n    ValidationErrorTypes  ErrorTypeGroup  // All validation-related types\n    PerformanceErrorTypes ErrorTypeGroup  // All performance-related types\n    StatusCodeErrorTypes  ErrorTypeGroup  // Status code types\n    MessageErrorTypes     ErrorTypeGroup  // Error message types\n    HeaderErrorTypes      ErrorTypeGroup  // Header validation types\n)\n```\n\n**Relationships**:\n- **Groups**: Related error type constants\n- **Provides**: Convenient access to error type categories\n- **Used by**: Error filtering and categorization\n\n## Type Conversion Flow\n\n### String to ValidationErrorType\n\n```go\n// Input: string\nerrorTypeStr := \"status_code\"\n\n// Convert to ValidationErrorType\nerrorType := ValidationErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n\n// Get string representation\nerrorType.String() // \"status_code\"\n\n// Get category\nerrorType.Category() // CategoryHTTP\n\n// Get description\nerrorType.Description() // \"HTTP status code validation\"\n\n// Use in ValidationError\nve := ValidationError{\n    ErrorType: errorType.String(),\n    Message:   \"Validation failed\",\n}\n```\n\n### ValidationError to Map/JSON\n\n```go\n// Create ValidationError\nve := ValidationError{\n    ErrorType: ErrorTypeStatusCode,\n    Message:   \"Expected 200 but got 404\",\n    Expected:  200,\n    Actual:    404,\n}\n\n// Convert to map\ndata := ve.ToMap()\n// map[string]interface{}{\n//     \"error_type\": \"status_code\",\n//     \"message\": \"Expected 200 but got 404\",\n//     \"expected\": 200,\n//     \"actual\": 404,\n// }\n\n// Convert to JSON\njsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(ve)\n// {\"error_type\":\"status_code\",\"message\":\"Expected 200 but got 404\",\"expected\":200,\"actual\":404}\n```\n\n### ValidationFormatter to ValidationError\n\n```go\n// Create ValidationFormatter\nformatter := NewValidationFormatter(ErrorTypeStatusCode).\n    WithExpected(200).\n    WithActual(404).\n    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\")\n\n// Build ValidationError\nve := formatter.Format()\n\n// Result is a ValidationError with all configured fields\n```\n\n## Error Type Validation\n\n### Validation Chain\n\n```go\n// 1. Create error type from string\nerrorType := ValidationErrorTypeFromString(\"status_code\")\n\n// 2. Validate the error type\nif err := errorType.Validate(); err != nil {\n    log.Printf(\"Invalid error type: %v\", err)\n}\n\n// 3. Get category information\ncategory := errorType.Category()\n\n// 4. Use in ValidationError\nve := ValidationError{\n    ErrorType: errorType.String(),\n    Message:   \"Validation failed\",\n}\n\n// 5. Validate the ValidationError\nif err := ve.Validate(); err != nil {\n    log.Printf(\"Invalid ValidationError: %v\", err)\n}\n```\n\n### Type Safety Patterns\n\n```go\n// Pattern 1: Use constants directly\nve := ValidationError{\n    ErrorType: ErrorTypeStatusCode,  // Type-safe constant\n    Message:   \"Failed\",\n}\n\n// Pattern 2: Use enum type\nerrorType := TypeStatusCode\nve := ValidationError{\n    ErrorType: errorType.String(),  // Convert enum to string\n    Message:   \"Failed\",\n}\n\n// Pattern 3: Validate user input\nerrorTypeStr := getUserInput()\nif !IsValidErrorType(errorTypeStr) {\n    return fmt.Errorf(\"invalid error type: %s\", errorTypeStr)\n}\nve := ValidationError{\n    ErrorType: errorTypeStr,\n    Message:   \"Failed\",\n}\n```\n\n## Integration Examples\n\n### Example 1: HTTP Status Code Validation\n\n```go\n// Validate status code\nresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(resp, 200)\n\nif !result.Valid {\n    // Create ValidationError using the builder\n    err := NewValidationFormatter(ErrorTypeStatusCode).\n        WithExpected(200).\n        WithActual(result.ActualCode).\n        WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n        Format()\n\n    // Log the error\n    log.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\n\n    // Or convert to JSON for API response\n    jsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(err.ToMap())\n}\n```\n\n### Example 2: Error Message Pattern Validation\n\n```go\n// Validate error message pattern\npattern := EnhancedErrorMessagePattern{\n    FieldNames: DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames(),\n    Pattern:    \"invalid.*token\",\n    MustContain: []string{\"expired\", \"invalid\"},\n}\n\nresult := ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails(responseBody, pattern)\n\nif !result.Valid {\n    // Create ValidationError with details\n    err := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n        ErrorTypeErrorMessagePattern,\n        \"invalid.*token\",\n        result.Message,\n        \"OAuth token validation\",\n        extractResponseSnippet(responseBody),\n        result.FieldName,\n        \"\",\n        nil,\n        \"Pattern did not match\",\n        \"\",\n        result.Issues,\n    )\n\n    log.Printf(\"Message validation failed: %v\", err)\n}\n```\n\n### Example 3: CORS Header Validation\n\n```go\n// Define expected CORS configuration\nconfig := &CORSConfig{\n    AllowOrigin:     \"https://example.com\",\n    AllowMethods:    \"GET, POST, OPTIONS\",\n    AllowHeaders:    \"Content-Type, Authorization\",\n    AllowCredentials: true,\n}\n\n// Validate CORS headers\nif !CORSHeadersIsValid(resp, config) {\n    // Create ValidationError\n    err := FormatValidationError(\n        ErrorTypeCORSHeaders,\n        \"CORS headers configured\",\n        \"CORS headers missing or invalid\",\n        \"GET /api/data\",\n        \"\",\n    )\n\n    log.Printf(\"CORS validation failed: %v\", err)\n}\n```\n\n## Best Practices\n\n### 1. Use Constants for Error Types\n\n❌ **Avoid**: Magic strings\n```go\nve := ValidationError{\n    ErrorType: \"status_code\",  // Typo-prone\n    Message:   \"Failed\",\n}\n```\n\n✅ **Prefer**: Type-safe constants\n```go\nve := ValidationError{\n    ErrorType: ErrorTypeStatusCode,  // Type-safe\n    Message:   \"Failed\",\n}\n```\n\n### 2. Validate Error Types\n\n❌ **Avoid**: Assuming input is valid\n```go\nfunc CreateError(errorType string) ValidationError {\n    return ValidationError{ErrorType: errorType}  // No validation\n}\n```\n\n✅ **Prefer**: Validate before use\n```go\nfunc CreateError(errorType string) (ValidationError, error) {\n    if err := ValidateErrorType(errorType); err != nil {\n        return ValidationError{}, err\n    }\n    return ValidationError{ErrorType: errorType}, nil\n}\n```\n\n### 3. Use Builder Pattern for Complex Errors\n\n❌ **Avoid**: Direct construction with many fields\n```go\nve := ValidationError{\n    ErrorType: \"status_code\",\n    Message:   \"Failed\",\n    Expected:  200,\n    Actual:    404,\n    Context:   \"GET /api/users\",\n    FieldName: \"\",\n    Location:  \"\",\n    // ... many more fields\n}\n```\n\n✅ **Prefer**: Builder pattern for readability\n```go\nve := NewValidationFormatter(ErrorTypeStatusCode).\n    WithExpected(200).\n    WithActual(404).\n    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n    Format()\n```\n\n### 4. Leverage Type Categories\n\n❌ **Avoid**: Manual category checking\n```go\nif errorType == \"status_code\" || errorType == \"content_type\" || errorType == \"cors_headers\" {\n    // Handle HTTP errors\n}\n```\n\n✅ **Prefer**: Category-based logic\n```go\nif IsHTTPErrorType(errorType) {\n    // Handle HTTP errors\n}\n```\n\n## Type Relationship Diagram\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                    ValidationError (Core)                    │\n│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\n│  │ ErrorType (string) ←── ErrorType constants          │   │\n│  │ Message   (string)                                   │   │\n│  │ Expected  (interface{})                              │   │\n│  │ Actual    (interface{})                              │   │\n│  │ ... optional fields ...                             │   │\n│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n                           │\n                           │ uses\n                           ↓\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│              ErrorType Constants & Enums                     │\n│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\n│  │ ErrorTypeStatusCode, ErrorTypeErrorMessage, ...     │   │\n│  │ (string constants from error_categories.go)          │   │\n│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\n│  │ TypeStatusCode, TypeErrorMessage, ...               │   │\n│  │ (ValidationErrorType enum from error_type_enum.go)  │   │\n│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n                           │\n                           │ categorizes\n                           ↓\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                    ErrorCategory (Enum)                      │\n│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\n│  │ CategoryHTTP, CategoryContent, CategoryValidation, │   │\n│  │ CategoryPerformance, CategorySecurity, CategoryCustom│   │\n│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n                           │\n                           │ creates\n                           ↓\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│              Builder & Helper Types                         │\n│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\n│  │ ValidationFormatter (builder pattern)                │   │\n│  │ FormatOption (functional options)                    │   │\n│  │ CORSConfig, ErrorResponseFieldNames, ...             │   │\n│  │ StatusCodeValidationResult, ErrorMessagePattern, ...│   │\n│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n                           │\n                           │ organizes\n                           ↓\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                 Error Type Collections                       │\n│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │\n│  │ ErrorTypeGroup, ValidationErrorTypes                 │   │\n│  │ HTTPErrorTypes, ContentErrorTypes, ...              │   │\n│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## Summary\n\nThe validation package provides a comprehensive type system for HTTP API validation:\n\n1. **ValidationError** is the core error representation\n2. **ErrorCategory** groups related error types\n3. **ValidationErrorType** provides strongly-typed error constants\n4. **Helper types** enable flexible validation scenarios\n5. **Builder types** provide ergonomic construction APIs\n6. **Result types** provide detailed validation information\n\nAll types work together to provide type-safe, flexible, and comprehensive validation error reporting for HTTP API testing and validation.\n","numLines":730,"startLine":1,"totalLines":730}}}
